steps.name: Implement collectConcernCandidates and list_concerns handler
steps.description: Replace the NOT_IMPLEMENTED stub for list_concerns in src/shell/server.ts with the real handler. Add collectConcernCandidates(input: ConcernCandidateInput): string[] and private helper extractFirstHeadingText(content: string): string | null to src/core/concern-matcher.ts per architecture-design.md contract: per-repo extraction returns feature directory names verbatim, ADR titles via extractFirstHeadingText with ADR-NNN: prefix stripped (filename-derived fallback if headingless), and heading text from every feature file via the existing detectHeadingLines. Add ListConcernsResponse type and formatListConcernsResponse formatter to src/core/format-response.ts. Wire the list_concerns handler in server.ts: loop over configured repos reusing resolve_concern's exact probe/skip pattern, accumulate candidates per repo via collectConcernCandidates, dedupe exact-string-match across the full accumulated list, cap at 200 entries in repo-config order with a truncation warning if exceeded, return searched_repos always populated, never an ERROR shape. Covers all 5 scenarios in list-concerns-discovery.feature.
criteria: list_concerns() returns candidate topics drawn from feature directory names and ADR titles across all configured repos
A repo with no nWave structure is silently excluded, not an error
All repos lacking nWave structure returns an empty concerns array, not an error
Duplicate topic signals across repos are deduplicated to one entry
Result capped at 200 entries with a truncation warning when exceeded
steps.scenario_name: Candidate topics are drawn from feature directories and ADR titles across repos

steps.name: Update README with list_concerns tool docs and close the gap
steps.description: Per architecture-design.md's Changes Per File table (FLAGGED FOR DELIVER ROADMAP): (1) Add a list_concerns() entry to README.md's '## MCP Tools' section, same format as the existing 3 tools (Input: none, Output: {concerns, searched_repos, warnings?}, no structured errors -- this tool never returns an ERROR shape). (2) Revise the 'Using LoreMCP while architecting' section's 'Known gap' paragraph (currently states list_concerns() is scoped but not yet built) to state the gap is CLOSED, and update the numbered workflow to recommend calling list_concerns() FIRST as the new browse step, before resolve_concern. This is part of this feature's delivery, not deferred documentation.
criteria: README's MCP Tools section documents list_concerns() with input/output shape matching the other 3 tools' documentation depth
README's 'Using LoreMCP while architecting' section no longer states list_concerns() is unbuilt -- the workflow is revised to call list_concerns() first
